text: It's January and that means allocation time! Several Sonoma County big names like Kistler, Occidental, and Rivers-Marie have all some very high scoring offerings for the 2023 and 2024 vintages as of this week. It will be interesting to see how 2024 compares to '23, which I have viewed to be really strong based on Sonoma Coast Pinot Noirs I've had from Hirsch, Ferren, Flowers, Blue Farm, DuMOL, RAEN, Failla, Marnet Cellars, and Alma de Cattleya. 2023 was marked by a cool and consistent growing season, bringing about well-ripen fruit. Would love to get my hands on some of Occidental's higher-tiered offerings like the 2023 Running Fence Vineyard Cuvee Catherine and SWK Vineyard, which received perfect 100 scores from Vinous and The Wine Palate. At $125 per bottle, not exactly cheap. Rivers-Marie's 2024 Sonoma Coast offering at $35 seems like great bargain. What's everyone excited for?
raw_body:
It's January and that means allocation time! Several Sonoma County big names like Kistler, Occidental, and Rivers-Marie have all some very high scoring offerings for the 2023 and 2024 vintages as of this week. It will be interesting to see how 2024 compares to '23, which I have viewed to be really strong based on Sonoma Coast Pinot Noirs I've had from Hirsch, Ferren, Flowers, Blue Farm, DuMOL, RAEN, Failla, Marnet Cellars, and Alma de Cattleya. 2023 was marked by a cool and consistent growing season, bringing about well-ripen fruit.

Would love to get my hands on some of Occidental's higher-tiered offerings like the 2023 Running Fence Vineyard Cuvee Catherine and SWK Vineyard, which received perfect 100 scores from Vinous and The Wine Palate. At $125 per bottle, not exactly cheap.

Rivers-Marie's 2024 Sonoma Coast offering at $35 seems like great bargain.

What's everyone excited for?
author: huckdr

text: I do cellar consulting, have 30 years in the biz, and would be happy to provide references and a bid. I work with customers on their preferred regions and flavor profile, and also on making a cellar/list that can match all food and guests' likes. I also work for an importer and can get amazing deals I pass on to clients. I'm in Santa Barbara County, CA. weswinesATgmailDOTcom
raw_body:
I do cellar consulting, have 30 years in the biz, and would be happy to provide references and a bid.  

I work with customers on their preferred regions and flavor profile, and also on making a cellar/list that can match all food and guests' likes.

I also work for an importer and can get amazing deals I pass on to clients.  I'm in Santa Barbara County, CA.

weswinesATgmailDOTcom
